steps.name: Wire the read-only IndexQueryPort into the viewer and render the verified object-search fragment
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (US-NS-001 capability + US-NS-002 render, N-1). Wire the slice-05 HttpIndexQueryAdapter (IndexQueryPort) into the `openlore ui` composition root in cli, resolving the indexer URL from the slice-05 seam (OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL / [appview] indexer_url, OD-NS-6, ADR-036) with NO signing key entering the viewer process. Add ViewerServer's Option<SharedIndexQuery> field + a bind_with_* constructor and thread it through the route table. Add GET /search to the route table with the Shape::from_request fork (read once). Add the pure SearchState ADT (Form | Results | NoResults | Unavailable) to viewer-domain plus render_search_results_fragment (the #search-results region, projecting appview-domain NetworkResultRow via the REUSED compose_results — per-author groups, verified + attributed, no merge) and render_search_page (chrome + public-data banner + GET form + the SAME fragment fn + the third nav link). Add the two xtask check-arch deltas: (a) add viewer-domain -> appview-domain to the pure-core dependency allowlist (pure->pure edge); (b) extend/confirm the viewer capability rule to admit the read-only IndexQueryPort (and, via cli, adapter-index-query) for adapter-http-viewer + viewer-domain while still forbidding any signing/identity/PDS crate and the indexer SERVER/store/ingest crates (adapter-xrpc-query-server / adapter-index-store / adapter-atproto-ingest). Add the start_with_indexer harness seam (mirrors start_with_github): seed_network_index(env, fixture) spawns a REAL slice-05 openlore-indexer serve and threads its url to the spawned openlore ui. Outcome: a reachable indexer + HX-Request -> ONLY the verified+attributed #search-results fragment. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN: the AT spawns BOTH the openlore (viewer) bin AND the openlore-indexer bin, so the run must cargo build both before executing these ATs.
criteria: GET /search with HX-Request over a reachable seeded indexer (object dimension, org.openlore.philosophy.reproducible-builds) returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #search-results region fragment (no page chrome, no <head>).

The fragment renders per-author groups; every row carries the author_did, the [verified] marker, and verbatim confidence (e.g. 0.85, never 0.9 or 90%) projected from appview-domain via the REUSED compose_results (no viewer-side grouping/verification path).

The openlore ui composition root resolves the indexer URL from OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL / [appview] indexer_url, wires HttpIndexQueryAdapter as Option<Arc<dyn IndexQueryPort>>, and holds NO signing key (no signing/identity/PDS surface enters the viewer process).

xtask check-arch passes with the two deltas: viewer-domain -> appview-domain is allowlisted as a pure->pure edge, and the viewer capability rule admits IndexQueryPort / adapter-index-query while still failing the build if adapter-http-viewer or viewer-domain links any signing/identity/PDS crate or the indexer SERVER/store/ingest crates.

viewer-domain remains pure (maud + ports + appview-domain only; no duckdb/tokio/reqwest/std::fs/std::net/SystemTime); the index-query soft probe at startup is informational and an unreachable indexer does NOT refuse viewer startup.

The start_with_indexer seam spawns a REAL openlore-indexer serve over the seeded fixture corpus and threads OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL to the spawned viewer; both the openlore and openlore-indexer bins are built before the AT runs.
acceptance_criteria: GET /search with HX-Request over a reachable seeded indexer (object dimension, org.openlore.philosophy.reproducible-builds) returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #search-results region fragment (no page chrome, no <head>).

The fragment renders per-author groups; every row carries the author_did, the [verified] marker, and verbatim confidence (e.g. 0.85, never 0.9 or 90%) projected from appview-domain via the REUSED compose_results (no viewer-side grouping/verification path).

The openlore ui composition root resolves the indexer URL from OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL / [appview] indexer_url, wires HttpIndexQueryAdapter as Option<Arc<dyn IndexQueryPort>>, and holds NO signing key (no signing/identity/PDS surface enters the viewer process).

xtask check-arch passes with the two deltas: viewer-domain -> appview-domain is allowlisted as a pure->pure edge, and the viewer capability rule admits IndexQueryPort / adapter-index-query while still failing the build if adapter-http-viewer or viewer-domain links any signing/identity/PDS crate or the indexer SERVER/store/ingest crates.

viewer-domain remains pure (maud + ports + appview-domain only; no duckdb/tokio/reqwest/std::fs/std::net/SystemTime); the index-query soft probe at startup is informational and an unreachable indexer does NOT refuse viewer startup.

The start_with_indexer seam spawns a REAL openlore-indexer serve over the seeded fixture corpus and threads OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL to the spawned viewer; both the openlore and openlore-indexer bins are built before the AT runs.
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (SearchState ADT + render_search_results_fragment projection over compose_results). Extend nightly mutation testing to this projection (verified marker, verbatim confidence, per-author no-merge) toward the Phase-5 >=80% gate.

steps.name: No-write surface, no-JS full page, and fragment/full-page parity
steps.description: Complete the walking-skeleton render contract (N-1b / N-2 / N-3). (N-1b) Assert the search capability exposes no write/sign surface on a Results response — no new write/sign/subscribe route, no executable follow control, no signing key in the process. (N-2) GET /search WITHOUT HX-Request serves a complete full page (chrome + public-data banner + GET dimension form + the embedded results region) for the no-JS no-regression path. (N-3) The #search-results fragment is structurally identical to the same region inside the full page (parity by construction — render_search_page EMBEDS render_search_results_fragment). This is the htmx + no-JS + parity trio of US-NS-001/002 collapsed into one TDD move.
criteria: A reachable object search with no HX-Request returns a complete full HTML page: <head>, page chrome, the public-data framing banner before results, the GET dimension form, and the embedded #search-results region.
The capability/response exposes NO write/sign surface: no new write/sign/subscribe route in the route inventory, no signing key read in the viewer process (I-NS-1).
The #search-results fragment returned under HX-Request is structurally identical to the results region embedded in the full page for the same query (parity by construction, I-NS-6).
The full-page response renders rows with the same author_did + [verified] + verbatim confidence as the fragment (no divergence between shapes).
acceptance_criteria: A reachable object search with no HX-Request returns a complete full HTML page: <head>, page chrome, the public-data framing banner before results, the GET dimension form, and the embedded #search-results region.
The capability/response exposes NO write/sign surface: no new write/sign/subscribe route in the route inventory, no signing key read in the viewer process (I-NS-1).
The #search-results fragment returned under HX-Request is structurally identical to the results region embedded in the full page for the same query (parity by construction, I-NS-6).
The full-page response renders rows with the same author_did + [verified] + verbatim confidence as the fragment (no divergence between shapes).

steps.name: Anti-merging for identical content and a guided no-results empty state
steps.description: Close out Phase 01 with the two correctness edges of US-NS-002 (N-4 / N-5). (N-4) Two different authors claiming identical content render as TWO rows under two author groups, never one merged consensus row — guaranteed structurally by REUSING compose_results (no viewer-side merge path). (N-5) A typo'd / unmatched object yields SearchState::NoResults rendering a plain-language guided empty state, never a blank region or crash.
criteria: An object search where two distinct authors publish identical-content verified claims renders TWO attributed rows (two author groups); NO merged or consensus row appears (anti-merging, I-NS-3).
Both rows carry their own author_did + [verified] marker; the rendering reuses compose_results (no second grouping/merge path exists in viewer-domain).
A reachable index queried for an object no indexed author matched yields SearchState::NoResults and renders a plain-language guided empty state.
The no-results state is not a blank region and does not crash or hang the viewer (boundary).
acceptance_criteria: An object search where two distinct authors publish identical-content verified claims renders TWO attributed rows (two author groups); NO merged or consensus row appears (anti-merging, I-NS-3).
Both rows carry their own author_did + [verified] marker; the rendering reuses compose_results (no second grouping/merge path exists in viewer-domain).
A reachable index queried for an object no indexed author matched yields SearchState::NoResults and renders a plain-language guided empty state.
The no-results state is not a blank region and does not crash or hang the viewer (boundary).
steps.mutation_note: The NoResults arm + the anti-merge projection are load-bearing pure logic; cover with mutation testing (the no-merged-row and guided-empty-state branches).

steps.name: Contributor search renders one author's verified trail with the honesty footer
steps.description: US-NS-003 contributor dimension (N-6 / N-7). Extend the dimension selector + handler to the contributor dimension, reusing the slice-05 handle->DID resolution (github:priya -> did:plc:priya-test#org.openlore.application). Render one developer's verified claims under a SINGLE author DID with the 'one developer's reasoning trail, not a community consensus' footer. The fragment/full-page parity move (N-7) is folded in.
criteria: A contributor search (github:priya) over a reachable index renders that one author's verified claims under a SINGLE author_did.
The contributor result shows the footer 'one developer's reasoning trail, not a community consensus'.
Every contributor row carries [verified] + the author DID + verbatim confidence (reusing the slice-05 handle->DID resolution, no new resolution path).
The contributor fragment (HX-Request) is structurally identical to the contributor results region in the full page (parity, I-NS-6).
acceptance_criteria: A contributor search (github:priya) over a reachable index renders that one author's verified claims under a SINGLE author_did.
The contributor result shows the footer 'one developer's reasoning trail, not a community consensus'.
Every contributor row carries [verified] + the author DID + verbatim confidence (reusing the slice-05 handle->DID resolution, no new resolution path).
The contributor fragment (HX-Request) is structurally identical to the contributor results region in the full page (parity, I-NS-6).
steps.mutation_note: The contributor projection (single-author-DID grouping + honesty footer) is pure render logic; cover with mutation testing.

steps.name: Subject search renders N author groups, never a consensus row
steps.description: US-NS-003 subject dimension (N-8 / N-9). Add the subject dimension; render a project's claims grouped BY AUTHOR (N distinct authors), each row [verified], with no merged 'the network thinks X about <subject>' consensus row (anti-merging guaranteed by reusing compose_results). The fragment/full-page parity move (N-9) is folded in.
criteria: A subject search (github:bazelbuild/bazel) over a reachable index renders N author groups (the distinct authors who claimed something about the subject).
Every subject row carries [verified] + its author DID; NO merged or consensus row about the subject appears (anti-merging, I-NS-3).
The grouping reuses compose_results (no viewer-side merge/grouping path).
The subject fragment (HX-Request) is structurally identical to the subject results region in the full page (parity, I-NS-6).
acceptance_criteria: A subject search (github:bazelbuild/bazel) over a reachable index renders N author groups (the distinct authors who claimed something about the subject).
Every subject row carries [verified] + its author DID; NO merged or consensus row about the subject appears (anti-merging, I-NS-3).
The grouping reuses compose_results (no viewer-side merge/grouping path).
The subject fragment (HX-Request) is structurally identical to the subject results region in the full page (parity, I-NS-6).

steps.name: Absent contributor renders a named no-suggestion empty state
steps.description: US-NS-003 absent-contributor edge (N-10) plus completing the dimension-selector form. A contributor handle no indexed author matches yields SearchState::NoResults that NAMES the queried handle with a plain-language empty state and offers NO near-match suggestion (an absent contributor is not a typo, unlike the object no-match in N-5). Finalize the GET form's dimension selector covering object / contributor / subject.
criteria: A contributor search (github:nobody-here) no indexed author matches yields SearchState::NoResults that names the queried handle in a plain-language empty state.
The absent-contributor empty state offers NO near-match suggestion (distinct from the object no-results state).
The /search GET form offers all three dimensions (object/philosophy, contributor, subject) and degrades to a plain navigation without JS.
The empty state does not crash or render a blank region (boundary).
acceptance_criteria: A contributor search (github:nobody-here) no indexed author matches yields SearchState::NoResults that names the queried handle in a plain-language empty state.
The absent-contributor empty state offers NO near-match suggestion (distinct from the object no-results state).
The /search GET form offers all three dimensions (object/philosophy, contributor, subject) and degrades to a plain navigation without JS.
The empty state does not crash or render a blank region (boundary).
steps.mutation_note: The dimension-aware NoResults branch (no-suggestion for contributor vs guided for object) is pure logic; cover with mutation testing.

steps.name: Public-data framing visible and counter-annotation shown-not-applied
steps.description: US-NS-004 trust surface (N-11 / N-12). (N-11) The /search page states up front, before any results, that discovery indexes only PUBLIC signed claims verified before indexing; every row carries [verified] + the author DID. (N-12) A claim that another author countered SHOWS its counter-annotation inline while the original claim is still rendered verbatim and is NOT merged or over-ridden (anti-merging — counter shown, never applied).
criteria: GET /search renders the public-data framing ('Discovery indexes only PUBLIC signed claims, verified before indexing. Nothing private is read.') before any results (I-NS-5).
Every rendered result row carries [verified] + the author DID (I-NS-3/4).
A row whose claim another author countered SHOWS the counter-annotation inline (e.g. 'countered by did:plc:maria').
The countered claim is still shown verbatim and is NOT merged, filtered, or over-ridden (counter shown-not-applied, I-NS-3).
acceptance_criteria: GET /search renders the public-data framing ('Discovery indexes only PUBLIC signed claims, verified before indexing. Nothing private is read.') before any results (I-NS-5).
Every rendered result row carries [verified] + the author DID (I-NS-3/4).
A row whose claim another author countered SHOWS the counter-annotation inline (e.g. 'countered by did:plc:maria').
The countered claim is still shown verbatim and is NOT merged, filtered, or over-ridden (counter shown-not-applied, I-NS-3).
steps.mutation_note: The counter-annotation shown-not-applied branch is load-bearing pure logic; cover with mutation testing (counter rendered AND claim not filtered).

steps.name: Unreachable indexer degrades to a calm payload-free notice in both shapes
steps.description: US-NS-004 degradation on an unreachable index (N-13 / N-14). A configured-but-unreachable indexer (ClosedIndexerPort, connection refused by construction) maps Err(Unreachable)/Err(_) to SearchState::Unavailable — a payload-free unit variant rendering the single pinned plain-language notice. Asserted in BOTH the full-page (N-13) and fragment (N-14) shapes, with no transport leak.
criteria: A configured-but-unreachable indexer (ClosedIndexerPort) maps to SearchState::Unavailable and renders the fixed plain-language notice ('The network index is unavailable... Your local store views still work.') in the FULL page (N-13).
The same posture under HX-Request renders the SAME fixed notice in the #search-results FRAGMENT (N-14).
Neither shape leaks transport internals: no HTTP status, no 'connection refused', no raw URL, no stack trace (assert_search_html_leaks_no_transport_internals).
The viewer does not crash or hang; SearchState::Unavailable is a payload-free unit variant (no transport string interpolated).
acceptance_criteria: A configured-but-unreachable indexer (ClosedIndexerPort) maps to SearchState::Unavailable and renders the fixed plain-language notice ('The network index is unavailable... Your local store views still work.') in the FULL page (N-13).
The same posture under HX-Request renders the SAME fixed notice in the #search-results FRAGMENT (N-14).
Neither shape leaks transport internals: no HTTP status, no 'connection refused', no raw URL, no stack trace (assert_search_html_leaks_no_transport_internals).
The viewer does not crash or hang; SearchState::Unavailable is a payload-free unit variant (no transport string interpolated).
steps.mutation_note: The Unavailable mapping (Err(_) -> payload-free Unavailable) is load-bearing no-leak logic; cover with mutation testing (the error arm collapses to the unit variant).

steps.name: Unconfigured indexer degrades, and unfollowed authors get CLI follow-guidance text
steps.description: US-NS-004 unconfigured degradation (N-15 / N-16) + read-only follow affordance (N-17). (N-15/16) An unconfigured indexer (OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL unset, no network call attempted) maps to SearchState::Unavailable rendering the same fixed notice in BOTH the full-page and fragment shapes. (N-17) An unfollowed-author row shows the 'openlore peer add <did>' guidance as plain TEXT and NO clickable/executable follow or subscribe control (the viewer stays read-only).
criteria: With OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL unset, a search renders SearchState::Unavailable (the fixed notice) WITHOUT attempting a network call, in the FULL page (N-15).
The same unconfigured posture under HX-Request renders the SAME fixed notice in the #search-results fragment (N-16).
Neither unconfigured shape leaks transport internals and the viewer does not crash (I-NS-2).
An unfollowed-author result row shows the 'openlore peer add <did>' guidance as plain TEXT and renders NO clickable/executable follow or subscribe control (I-NS-1 / WD-NS-3, N-17).
acceptance_criteria: With OPENLORE_INDEXER_URL unset, a search renders SearchState::Unavailable (the fixed notice) WITHOUT attempting a network call, in the FULL page (N-15).
The same unconfigured posture under HX-Request renders the SAME fixed notice in the #search-results fragment (N-16).
Neither unconfigured shape leaks transport internals and the viewer does not crash (I-NS-2).
An unfollowed-author result row shows the 'openlore peer add <did>' guidance as plain TEXT and renders NO clickable/executable follow or subscribe control (I-NS-1 / WD-NS-3, N-17).
steps.mutation_note: The unconfigured (None index_query) -> Unavailable branch and the render-only follow-text helper are pure logic; cover with mutation testing.

steps.name: Gold: every search route leaves the store read-only
steps.description: N-INV-ReadOnly (I-NS-8 / WD-NS-7). Exercise EVERY /search route across EVERY dimension (object / contributor / subject) AND both shapes (full page + htmx fragment) over a reachable index, and assert the local DuckDB store row counts (claims + peer_claims) are UNCHANGED via the slice-06 universe-bound assert_store_read_only (Mandate 8). Network READ persists nothing.
criteria: Exercising /search across all three dimensions (object, contributor, subject) and both shapes (full page + fragment) over a reachable index leaves claims.row_count UNCHANGED.
The same exercise leaves peer_claims.row_count UNCHANGED.
The assertion uses the slice-06 capture_store_row_count_universe + assert_store_read_only (Mandate 8 universe = the two port-exposed counts, all 'unchanged').
No /search code path issues a store write (results computed per query; zero new persisted types).
acceptance_criteria: Exercising /search across all three dimensions (object, contributor, subject) and both shapes (full page + fragment) over a reachable index leaves claims.row_count UNCHANGED.
The same exercise leaves peer_claims.row_count UNCHANGED.
The assertion uses the slice-06 capture_store_row_count_universe + assert_store_read_only (Mandate 8 universe = the two port-exposed counts, all 'unchanged').
No /search code path issues a store write (results computed per query; zero new persisted types).

steps.name: Gold: no search response adds a write or sign control
steps.description: N-INV-NoWrite (I-NS-1 / WD-NS-3 / I-SCR-1). Assert that no /search response shape (full page or fragment, over a reachable index) renders a sign / publish / subscribe / executable-follow control on the observable rendered surface across every shape. The only follow affordance is the render-only 'peer add' TEXT (asserted in N-17).
criteria: No /search full-page response renders a sign, publish, subscribe, or executable-follow control (no <button>/<form>/hx-* follow affordance).
No /search fragment response renders any such control.
The assertion holds over a reachable index across the observable rendered surface for every shape.
The only follow affordance present anywhere is the render-only 'openlore peer add <did>' TEXT (the human gate stays in the CLI, I-NS-1).
acceptance_criteria: No /search full-page response renders a sign, publish, subscribe, or executable-follow control (no <button>/<form>/hx-* follow affordance).
No /search fragment response renders any such control.
The assertion holds over a reachable index across the observable rendered surface for every shape.
The only follow affordance present anywhere is the render-only 'openlore peer add <did>' TEXT (the human gate stays in the CLI, I-NS-1).

steps.name: Gold: the search page chrome stays offline (no CDN)
steps.description: N-INV-OfflineChrome (I-NS-7 / KPI-HX-G2). Assert the /search full page references ONLY the LOCAL /static/htmx.min.js script src and NO off-host CDN reference — the page chrome stays offline-capable even though the search itself needs the network (exactly like /scrape).
criteria: The /search full page's only <script src> is the local /static/htmx.min.js (the vendored, SHA-256-pinned slice-07 asset).
The /search page contains NO off-host / CDN script or asset reference.
The offline-chrome guarantee holds independent of whether the search itself reaches the network.
acceptance_criteria: The /search full page's only <script src> is the local /static/htmx.min.js (the vendored, SHA-256-pinned slice-07 asset).
The /search page contains NO off-host / CDN script or asset reference.
The offline-chrome guarantee holds independent of whether the search itself reaches the network.

steps.name: Gold: every rendered search row is verified by construction
steps.description: N-INV-Verified (I-NS-4 / KPI-AV-3). Across the dimension surface (object / contributor / subject) over a reachable index, assert EVERY rendered result row carries [verified] + an author DID and NO [unverified] / 'unknown signature' state ever appears — verified-by-construction (the indexer is the verify gate; the viewer has no second verification path).
criteria: Across object, contributor, and subject searches over a reachable index, every rendered result row carries the [verified] marker AND an author DID.
No rendered row ever shows an [unverified] / 'unknown signature' / unverified state (no such state exists in the viewer).
The assertion uses assert_search_html_every_row_verified_and_attributed across all dimensions.
Verification is by construction (the viewer renders already-verified indexer results; there is no second verification path).
acceptance_criteria: Across object, contributor, and subject searches over a reachable index, every rendered result row carries the [verified] marker AND an author DID.
No rendered row ever shows an [unverified] / 'unknown signature' / unverified state (no such state exists in the viewer).
The assertion uses assert_search_html_every_row_verified_and_attributed across all dimensions.
Verification is by construction (the viewer renders already-verified indexer results; there is no second verification path).
